channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Is season running away from frazzled Arsenal?
item.description: Arsenal must regroup after their hopes of a quadruple are wrecked in two weeks by successive defeats for the first time this season.

item.title: Survival or FA Cup glory - which would you choose?
item.description: Leeds visit West Ham on Sunday in the FA Cup quarter-finals but both are also in a relegation scrap. Would you risk safety for glory?

item.title: Win no fluke, says Shearer, as Eckert's Saints target Wembley treble
item.description: Fifty years after their one and only FA Cup success, underdogs Southampton are again stunning top-flight opposition - and doing so in style.

item.title: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
item.description: Nobody would have believed that 12 months on from Liverpool's title win, a fanbase so united behind its team and its manager would be, at best, apathetic as to whether Arne Slot remains in the job.

item.title: Haaland's 'old-school' lesson for Liverpool as FA Cup record falls
item.description: Manchester City have beaten Arsenal and taken apart Liverpool in their past two games and Erling Haaland's return to goalscoring form has laid down the gauntlet for the run-in.

item.title: Rohl won't watch title rivals as Rangers enjoy top spot
item.description: As Rangers move to the top of the Scottish Premiership for the first time this season, head coach Danny Rohl is "convinced" they can finish the job.

item.title: Southampton shock Arsenal to reach FA Cup semi-finals
item.description: Arsenal are stung by a late goal from Southampton's Shea Charles, as the Premier League leaders fall to defeat in the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Why Falkirk's boss could be manager of year in Scotland - again
item.description: John McGlynn is a man with the Midas touch as Falkirk secure a top-six finish on their return to the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: Chelsea chase Strasbourg defender - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Chelsea seek defender from partner club, Inter target Spurs goalkeeper and Liverpool look to add to defensive ranks, plus more.

item.title: Ex-Chelsea player Oscar retires with heart issue
item.description: Former Chelsea and Brazil midfielder Oscar retires after being diagnosed with a heart condition.

item.title: Could Aberdeen really be relegated for first time?
item.description: Following Saturday's defeat at fellow strugglers St Mirren, Aberdeen are in serious danger of finishing in the relegation play-off spot. Do they have what it takes to stay up?

item.title: Charles scores late winner as Southampton beat Arsenal
item.description: Southampton secure a spot in the FA Cup semi-finals thanks to Shea Charles' winner in the 85th minute.

item.title: Rashford strikes as Barcelona beat Atletico Madrid
item.description: England forward Marcus Rashford scores an impressive goal in Barcelona's 2-1 win against Atletico Madrid.

item.title: Full-time scenes as Southampton beat Arsenal in FA Cup
item.description: St Mary's Stadium erupts into celebration as Southampton beat Arsenal to book a place in the FA Cup semi finals.

item.title: 'It stays in the dressing room' - Arteta coy after Arsenal loss
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta reacts to Arsenal's 2-1 loss to Southampton in the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Haaland hat-trick sinks lacklustre Liverpool
item.description: Manchester City's Erling Haaland scores a hat-trick in the 4-0 victory against Liverpool, which sends Pep Guardiola's side to the FA Cup semi-finals for an eighth successive season.

item.title: How former Liverpool prospect found love for football again
item.description: After 18 years at Liverpool, Tyler Morton tells BBC Sport how moving to Lyon in the summer has helped him fall back in love with football.

item.title: Ex-England defender Trippier to leave Newcastle
item.description: Kieran Trippier is to leave Newcastle United when the defender's contract expires this summer.

item.title: After more than 14 years at Atletico Madrid, what next for Simeone?
item.description: BBC Sport asks if Atletico Madrid could really part ways with manager Diego Simeone at the end of the season?

item.title: Football rally in Peru leaves one dead and dozens injured
item.description: Hundreds of Alianza Lima fans had gathered for a pregame rally ahead of a match against local rivals Universitario de Deportes.

item.title: O'Neill wary of dropped points as games run out for Celtic
item.description: Martin O'Neill says further dropped points by Celtic would make winning the Scottish Premiership "a difficult comeback" but "not impossible".

item.title: Chelsea thrash Port Vale 7-0 to reach FA Cup semi-final
item.description: Chelsea score seven goals against League One strugglers Port Vale at Stamford Bridge to reach the FA Cup semi-finals.

item.title: White error as Stewart gives Southampton shock lead over Arsenal
item.description: Ross Stewart capitalises on a "misjudgement" by Ben White to give Southampton a shock lead against Premier League leaders Arsenal in the FA Cup.

item.title: Bayern Munich's stunning comeback in 99th minute
item.description: Bundesliga leaders Bayern Munich came back from two goals down to clinch a victory over Freiburg thanks to Lennart Karl's 99th-minute winner.

item.title: There is no ceiling for Estevao - Rosenior
item.description: Manager Liam Rosenior says there is "no ceiling" for Estevao after the Brazilian teenager's impressive performance in Chelsea's 7-0 victory against Port Vale.

item.title: 'It's ridiculous' - Haaland hails Guardiola's Wembley record
item.description: Manchester City's Erling Haaland says Pep Guardiola's record of reaching Wembley 23 times in 10 seasons at Manchester City is "ridiculous".

item.title: What De Zerbi needs to do to turn Tottenham around
item.description: Tony Pulis explores why Tottenham are in such a mess and what their new manager Roberto de Zerbi might do to change things as they seek to stay in the Premier League.

item.title: Sutton's FA Cup predictions v boxer Richard Riakporhe
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxer Richard Riakporhe plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: McArdle out to end NI's goal drought - could Chambers help?
item.description: Northern Ireland boss Michael McArdle talks of his side's goalscoring issues as well as the impact uncapped striker Cora Chambers could make to solving those problems.

item.title: Gattuso leaves Italy role - who could replace him?
item.description: Manager Gennaro Gattuso and Italy agree to "mutually terminate" his contract after their failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup.

item.title: Wrexham back in top six as Parkinson to send dossier to head of referees
item.description: Phil Parkinson says he will send a compilation of incidents to referees in a bid to clear up his frustrations over some recent decisions involving his Wrexham side.

item.title: Liverpool defeat to Man City really damaging - Murphy
item.description: Former Liverpool midfielder Danny Murphy describes Liverpool's 4-0 FA Cup defeat against Manchester City as "really damaging" and says their second-half performance "asked a lot of questions".

item.title: Barcelona run riot against Madrid to reach Champions League semis
item.description: Barcelona cruise into the Women's Champions League semi-finals, beating Real Madrid 6-0 at the Nou Camp and 12-2 on aggregate, setting up a tie against Bayern Munich.

item.title: Arsenal reach Champions League semis as Chelsea's Bompastor sent off
item.description: Arsenal lose 1-0 in the second leg of their Women's Champions League quarter-final against Chelsea but win 3-2 on aggregate to reach the semi-finals and move a step closer to successfully defending their title.

item.title: McCabe avoids red after hair pull on Thompson
item.description: Watch the moment Katie McCabe appears to pull Alyssa Thompson's hair during Chelsea's 1-0 win against Arsenal in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final.

item.title: 'My players don't deserve this' - Bompastor furious after hair pull
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor says her players "deserve better" after she was sent off during her side's 1-0 win against Arsenal in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final, in a match which saw Katie McCabe appear to pull the hair of Chelsea's Alyssa Thompson.

item.title: Bayern strike late to knock Manchester United out of Champions League
item.description: Manchester United go out of the Women's Champions League as Bayern Munich score two late goals in the second leg of their quarter-final to win 2-1 on the night and 5-3 on aggregate.

item.title: 'You can't be doing that' - pundits react to McCabe hair pull
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Ellen White and Steph Houghton react to Katie McCabe's hair pull on Alyssa Thompson as Chelsea beat Arsenal 1-0 in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final, but lose the tie 3-2 on aggregate.

item.title: Little wants more Champions League success as Arsenal reach final four
item.description: Arsenal captain Kim Little says she believes her side are "getting better and better" as they reach the semi-finals of the Women's Champions League, after beating Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate to close in on successfully defending their title.

item.title: I can't expect to jump in at top level - Defoe
item.description: Former England striker Jermain Defoe hopes he can secure promotion with National League side Woking as the 43-year-old replaces Neal Ardley to take on his first managerial role.

item.title: Watch: Scotland lose friendly against Ivory Coast
item.description: Watch the best of the action as Scotland are beaten 1-0 by Ivory Coast at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium.

item.title: Wiegman on 'speechless' Parkinson's England call-up
item.description: England manager Sarina Wiegman explains why she has handed a first senior call-up to 17-year-old midfielder Erica Meg Parkinson for World Cup qualifiers with Spain and Iceland.

item.title: Is Scottish Premiership title race set for fresh twist?
item.description: After a two-week break from the carnage, what's next in one of the most gripping title fights in Europe?

item.title: De Zerbi apologises to fans for Greenwood comments
item.description: New Tottenham boss Roberto de Zerbi apologises to fans over comments he made about Mason Greenwood.

item.title: The Championship run-in: 24 key questions for 24 clubs
item.description: BBC Sport breaks down the main storylines and talking points as the Championship enters the final section of the 2025-26 season.

item.title: The $11k World Cup final ticket - what we learned from first open sale
item.description: Fifa is charging what is thought to be the most expensive general admission price for a football match.

item.title: Kerr's uncertain future - should Chelsea keep striker?
item.description: The future of Chelsea striker Sam Kerr remains uncertain - but would it be a wise decision to let her leave on a free transfer this summer?

item.title: Is success crucial? BBC Sport experts pick their player of the season
item.description: With award season just around the corner, BBC Sport experts pick their contenders for Premier League player of the year. Do you agree?

item.title: The remarkable rise of Tuchel's English assistant
item.description: Ten years after playing for Accrington, Anthony Barry is hoping to help Thomas Tuchel win the World Cup for England.

item.title: The World Cup line-up is complete - here's what you need to know
item.description: The final six nations have qualified for the 2026 World Cup - this is your essential lowdown on the expanded edition of the tournament.

item.title: BBC Sport's McNulty picks his England World Cup squad - do you agree?
item.description: BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty picks his 26-man England World Cup squad - do you agree?

item.title: Forget Forest & Spurs... Barrow are the kings of chaos
item.description: Now onto their fifth manager of the season, are League Two Barrow the true 'kings of chaos'?

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup quarter-finals on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: How does Tudor's record compare to other Tottenham bosses?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team compare Igor Tudor's managerial record to other Tottenham managers of recent years.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: Rangers track forward Gelhardt - gossip
item.description: Rangers could use 49ers connections to land Leeds striker and Hearts star on Portugal radar...

item.title: Napoli warn they could punish Lukaku over training no-show
item.description: Striker Romelu Lukaku could face disciplinary action after Napoli released a statement to say he has ignored a request to return to the club.

item.title: Griezmann to join MLS side Orlando from Atletico
item.description: Former France forward Antoine Griezmann will join MLS side Orlando City from Atletico Madrid this summer.

item.title: PSG's league game between legs of Liverpool Champions League tie postponed
item.description: Paris St-Germain's Ligue One match against Lens, which was set to take place between the two legs of their Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool, has been postponed.

item.title: How Arbeloa is getting the best out of Real Madrid's stars
item.description: BBC Sport looks at how Alvaro Arbeloa has managed to get the best out of the Real Madrid squad since his appointment in January.

item.title: Real talk is nonsense but I may coach again - Klopp
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp says speculation he will return to management with Real Madrid this summer is "nonsense".

item.title: Lang's surgery for thumb injury 'went well'
item.description: Galatasaray midfielder Noa Lang is set to have surgery in Liverpool after suffering a "serious cut" at Anfield.

item.title: How WSL 2 side Charlton are defying odds in league and cup
item.description: Hardly anyone was talking about Charlton being WSL 2 title contenders before the season began. But they are defying the odds - and also have a cup quarter-final to look forward to.

item.title: Uncapped Chambers named in McArdle's first NI squad
item.description: Uncapped striker Cora Chambers is included in new Northern Ireland boss Michael McArdle's first squad for their April World Cup qualifying double-header against Malta.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
